Editor's Note

Introduction

Hi, I'm Hriday — an MBA student at Bennett University with a hands-on obsession for brand building, content strategy, and campaign execution across both D2C and B2B environments.

My internship work spans two very different brand worlds: HUES FOR HIM, a premium D2C men's grooming label, and GoodLucky, a legacy stationery brand repositioning itself for a Gen Z audience. Between the two, I've planned launches, written copy, built creative direction for shoots and reels, and run campaigns meant to make a brand impossible to scroll past.

I care less about content that performs once, and more about brand systems that compound — the kind of positioning, tone, and visual language an audience starts to recognize before they even see the logo.

— Hriday

Hriday Ahuja — Greater Noida, India

Capabilities

My approach to brand building

Brand Positioning · Content Strategy · Campaign Development · Social Media Growth · Influencer Marketing · Creative Direction · Performance Content · Copywriting · Market Research

Chapter One

Hues For Him

HUES FOR HIM — Case Study 01

Brand Positioning

Establish HUES FOR HIM as a premium, editorial-grade grooming brand for men — not a novelty, but a genuine daily ritual.

Built brand-first content around bold serif typography and lifestyle imagery — a man getting ready for his day, product laid out like an everyday essential beside his keys, phone, and watch. Extended the same restrained, elevated visual language into seasonal creative like "Hues of Summer" to keep the brand feeling premium across contexts, not just product-forward.

View on Instagram ↗ View on Instagram ↗
HUES FOR HIM — Case Study 02

Liquid Foundation Launch & Social Proof

Normalize makeup for Indian men by launching the Liquid Foundation across four inclusive shades, and build trust through real user validation.

Paired an editorial shade campaign — "Four Shades. Every Man." — with authentic customer review carousels tracking real buyer language from first impression to one-month verdict, proving efficacy over hard-selling it. Extended reach through influencer content and moody, high-production video to keep the product desirable rather than clinical.

Carousel ↗ Carousel ↗ Video ↗ Reel ↗ Reel ↗
HUES FOR HIM — Case Study 03

The Father's Day Campaign

Drive giveaway participation and gifting-season sales for the Color Changing Moisturiser by repositioning the brand as "Hues For Dad."

Built a teaser-to-reveal arc — a minimal "zoom in for something special" post to build curiosity, followed by the giveaway reveal — supported by an office reel showing the team's own playful, relatable energy around the launch.

Result 200+ giveaway entries
View on Instagram ↗ View on Instagram ↗ Reel ↗
Influencer Partnership — Profile

Sarabjeet Singh × Hues For Him

Reach a broader, culturally engaged male audience and lend the brand social credibility through a recognized creator.

Ran a paid partnership built around a simple, native routine format — Sarabjeet applying the product on camera — keeping the content feeling like genuine personal routine content rather than an ad, with a comment-to-share mechanic to extend organic reach.

Reel ↗

Chapter Two

GoodLucky Stationery

GOODLUCKY — Case Study 01

Brand Awareness Carousels

Reposition GoodLucky from "just a school notebook" to a creativity tool relevant to an older, design- and idea-driven audience.

Used a myth-busting hook format — "99% of students use their notebook wrong" and "Why your brain remembers what your screen forgets" — to earn attention with a contrarian claim, then resolved it with the brand's actual positioning: that writers, designers, architects, and founders still reach for a notebook over a screen.

Carousel ↗ Carousel ↗
GOODLUCKY — Case Study 02

Organic Reels Strategy

Build a humor- and nostalgia-driven organic content engine that makes GoodLucky feel culturally relevant to Gen Z — not just a legacy stationery brand.

Used relatable meme formats and emotional storytelling in equal measure — nostalgia-driven "POV" reels about old notebooks, comedic hooks like "if multitasking was an Olympic sport", and an emotional brand film — to build a varied, high-engagement content mix rather than repetitive product posts. This approach extended to paid/organic hybrid ad content.

Result 1.6M views on one ad execution
Reel ↗ Reel ↗ Reel ↗ Reel ↗ Reel ↗
1.6M views ↗
